lessonTitle: Treating Pain at Home and Targeting Pathobiology of Vaso-Occlusive Episodes in Sickle Cell Disease (SCD)

items.html: Review current gold standard of therapy of vaso-occlusive episodes
Identify research studies focused on targeting the pathobiology of Sickle Cell Disease
Discuss how to treat vaso-occlusive episodes at home

slides.title: Current Gold Standard Therapy for Adults

data.html:
To start our innovations in Sickle Cell journey, let's start with work that is being done around treating VOEs at home.

Drepadom - a Homecare Protocol for Vaso-Occlusive Crisis for Sickle Cell Disease Patients (Pelinski et al.)

DREPADOM is a home-care protocol for VOC management for low-risk patients.

Risk is determined by the validated PRESEV score (Bartolucci – eBioMed 2016) that evaluates the risk of developing Acute Chest Syndrome (ACS).

Eligibility for DREPADOM: • Patients arriving at the ER for VOC with a PRESEV score ≤ 6 and not presenting any exclusion criteria • Patients discharged from at least 3 days of hospitalization for VOC and/or ACS

Coordinator coordinates delivery of PO/parenteral treatments (including opioids), and home oxygen, to the patients' home within four hours.

Homecare nurse performs house calls twice a day.

Daily review of decision-making algorithm supervised by SCD experts to determine stopping/continuing outpatient follow-up vs. rehospitalization.

Of 209 patients (105 from ED and 104 from Early Release), 22 (11%) were re-admitted to hospital for uncontrolled/chest pain, ACS, and infections. Study conclusion: Drepadom is a successful and safe tool to reduce hospital stay for SCD patients.

slides.title: Targeting the Pathobiology of Sickle Cell Disease Current Innovations

data.html: Instead of focusing on treating the pain, let's look at innovations that are focused on targeting the pathobiology of SCD to prevent pain/complications.

To get us started, let's jump right to the innovations focused on targeting VOCs.

Crizanlizumab

  • Approved by FDA in 2019

Mechanism:

  • Monoclonal antibody for P-selectin, which is expressed on the surface of endothelial cells and platelets and promotes adhesion

Evidence:

  • SUSTAIN trial (Phase II)
  • High dose vs placebo had reduction in VOCs but no change in hospitalization rates

Cost:

  • Estimated $96,000/year

Photo credit: (Salinas Cisneros G, & Thein SL., 2020); Karki, N & Kutlar, A. (2021)

items.html: Research is being done to find ways to treat vaso-occlusive episodes at home, to improve quality of life for those living with SCD
The pathobiology of SCD can be targeted towards vaso-occlusion, inflammation, Hb S polymerization, and changing the genotype
There are promising treatments on the horizon that are not looking at ways of treating the pain, but instead are looking for ways to prevent the pain from happening in the 1st place
